title: [firrtl] extmodules with HasBlackBoxInline instantiated only in a layer not emitted into layers location
body:
What I expect to happen:
------------------------

When I create an extmodule and generate it using HasBlackBoxInline annotation and it is instantiated only in a Layer, I expect that module to be emitted into the place where that layer belongs (e.g. in the TestBenchDirAnnotation-indicated directory when --extract-test-code is run).


What actually happens:
----------------------

Note that this works correctly for FIRRTL modules instantiated only in layers. It doesnt' work for those generated with HasBlackBoxInline.

For HasBlackBoxInline, they are generated alongside the non-layer code.

Steps to reproduce:
-------------------

```
bash-4.4$ firtool --version
LLVM (http://llvm.org/):
  LLVM version 19.0.0git
  Optimized build.
CIRCT firtool-1.70.0
```

in example.fir:
```
FIRRTL version 4.0.0

circuit FooTestbench: %[[
 {    "class":"sifive.enterprise.firrtl.MarkDUTAnnotation",
    "target":"FooTestbench.Foo"
 },
 {
    "class":"firrtl.transforms.DontTouchAnnotation",
    "target":"~FooTestbench|Foo>in"
  },
   {
    "class":"firrtl.transforms.DontTouchAnnotation",
    "target":"~FooTestbench|Foo>out"
  },
   {
    "class":"firrtl.transforms.DontTouchAnnotation",
    "target":"~FooTestbench|FirrtlLogger>in_snoop"
  },
   {
    "class":"firrtl.transforms.DontTouchAnnotation",
    "target":"~FooTestbench|FirrtlLogger>out_snoop"
  },
  {
    "class":"firrtl.transforms.BlackBoxInlineAnno",
    "target":"FooTestbench.BBLogger",
    "name":"BBLogger.sv",
    "text":"Black Box Logger verilog goes here!\n"
  },
  {
    "class": "sifive.enterprise.firrtl.TestBenchDirAnnotation",
    "dirname": "testbench"
  }
]]  
  
  layer A, bind :

  extmodule BBLogger:
    input in_snoop: UInt<1>
    input out_snoop: UInt<1>

  module FirrtlLogger:
    input in_snoop: UInt<1>
    input out_snoop: UInt<1>
    
  module Foo:
    input in: UInt<1>
    output out: UInt<1>
    connect out, in

    layerblock A:
      inst bb_logger of BBLogger
      connect bb_logger.in_snoop, in
      connect bb_logger.out_snoop, out

      inst firrtl_logger of FirrtlLogger
      connect firrtl_logger.in_snoop, in
      connect firrtl_logger.out_snoop, out

  public module FooTestbench enablelayer A:
    inst foo of Foo
    foo.in is invalid
```

running:

```
 firtool --split-verilog --o=firrtool_outputs3 --extract-test-code  example.fir
```

gives:

```
bash-4.4$ tree
.
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ BBLogger.sv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ extern_modules.sv
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ filelist.f
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ firrtl_black_box_resource_files.f
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Foo.sv
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ testbench
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirrtlLogger.sv
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ Foo_A.sv
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FooTestbench.sv
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ layers_FooTestbench_A.sv
```

I would have expected `BBLogger.sv` to be a sibling to `FirrtlLogger.sv`.

Note that there is no issue in the actual code, BBLogger is correctly only bound-in in `layers_FooTestbench_A.sv`, this is just a file location issue.
